description:
Final sliver of VR.3: typed slots in closure heap payloads.

## Why

A closure capturing one int + one float is currently a fixed 3-slot FzValue payload (closure arity + 2 captures = 3 * 8 bytes inside the heap object, all tagged). After .5.4 it's a payload sized by the captures' Descrs: i64 raw slot for int + f64 raw slot for float (still 16 bytes here because both happen to be 8-wide, but the storage is raw — the win is per-op, not per-byte).

Captures are EXPLICIT (the IrClosure variant in fz_ir spells out the captured Vars) and IMMUTABLE (closure payloads never mutate after construction), so no escape analysis is needed. Build-time we know exactly what types flow into each slot.

## Adds

- `fz_closure_finalize` (runtime) accepts per-slot kinds + writes typed bytes — extends the existing fz_closure_begin/push API or replaces it with a typed variant.
- lower_prim MakeClosure / closure-construction: emits per-capture typed pushes using the FnTypes-derived kinds.
- `fz_closure_invoke` / closure-arg unpack: reads typed slots, producing raw values in the callee fn's entry frame slots (which are already raw post-.5.1/.5.2).

## Kills

- `(n_captures as u32) * SLOT_BYTES` uniform-payload-size assumption in fz_closure_begin.
- The "each captured slot is one FzValue" assumption inside the closure-payload GC tracer (already kind-aware in heap.rs if we declare a Schema for closure payloads — verify the closure path uses one).

## Acceptance

- Fixture: a closure capturing one int + one float, invoked across a tail call. CLIF asserts the closure-construction site doesn't go through `box_int` / `fz_alloc_float` for the captures (the raw bytes go straight in).
- GC fixtures still green (typed closure-payload tracer skips raw slots).
- Matrix green.

## Depends on

- fz-ul4.27.5.3 (continuations).

## Note re VR.4

VR.4 (per-fn native ABI for direct calls — fz-ul4.27.6) overlaps the closure-invoke path: a closure ultimately calls a closed-over fn. If VR.4 lands first the invocation goes through a typed ABI naturally. Either ordering works; this ticket can land before VR.4 with the existing uniform `extern "C" fn(*mut u8, *mut u8) -> *mut u8` calling convention, doing per-slot typed read on the callee side.

## UNBLOCKS

- fz-ul4.27.5 (parent VR.3) closes once this lands
title: VR.3.5: typed slots in closure heap payloads
